questions.text: Why is resurrection described as the 'Great News'?
questions.answer: Because it is the most important reality people dispute, and it changes the meaning of life.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests it is normal to argue about the Hereafter without ever seeking evidence.
questions.answer: False. It answers doubts with clear signs and warnings.

questions.text: How does Surah An-Naba use the earth and mountains as evidence of Allah’s power?
questions.answer: It shows stability and design in creation, proving Allah can recreate and judge.

questions.text: What is one lesson from sleep being described as rest?
questions.answer: Daily rest is a mercy and a reminder that humans are weak and dependent on Allah.

questions.text: How do night and day in this Surah teach balance in life?
questions.answer: Night is for covering and rest; day is for work—both are signs of Allah’s mercy.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that humans created the order of night and day by their own power.
questions.answer: False. It presents them as Allah’s signs.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach by describing the heavens as strong and structured?
questions.answer: That creation is precise and powerful, pointing to a wise Creator and inevitable return.

questions.text: What does the Surah’s mention of rain producing food teach about provision?
questions.answer: Allah provides sustenance through ordered means, so gratitude and obedience are required.

questions.text: How does Surah An-Naba describe the Day of Sorting in a way that creates urgency?
questions.answer: It says it has an appointed time and people will be gathered in crowds.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the changes of the sky and mountains on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: The world’s stability will dissolve, proving the Day’s reality and Allah’s power.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Hell is only for those who make small mistakes.
questions.answer: False. It describes Hell as a return for transgressors who deny and persist.

questions.text: Why are transgressors punished severely according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Because they denied accountability and rejected Allah’s signs, living without preparing for return.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Allah recording deeds?
questions.answer: Everything is counted and preserved; no deed is lost.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe Paradise as 'success' for the righteous?
questions.answer: It is safety and reward: gardens, comfort, pure speech, and lasting honor.

questions.text: What is one moral lesson from the absence of vain talk and lies in Paradise?
questions.answer: Pure environments are built on truth; lying and nonsense are signs of corrupted hearts.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that speaking truth is only important in religious settings.
questions.answer: False. It makes truthfulness a mark of the righteous outcome.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about angels standing in rows?
questions.answer: It shows the Day’s seriousness and order; even angels do not speak except by Allah’s permission.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about taking a path back to Allah?
questions.answer: People must choose repentance and obedience now, before the True Day arrives.

questions.text: How can a believer apply Surah An-Naba’s message about daily signs?
questions.answer: Use sleep, work, food, and nature as reminders to pray, repent, and stay grateful.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention a person wishing to be dust?
questions.answer: To show the extreme regret of the disbeliever when consequences become undeniable.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that regret after seeing the truth will still save a person.
questions.answer: False. Regret without prior repentance comes too late.

questions.text: How does Surah An-Naba strengthen belief in Allah without complex arguments?
questions.answer: It points to obvious signs—earth, sky, sleep, rain—and connects them to resurrection.

questions.text: How can a modern reader use this Surah as a weekly reset?
questions.answer: Reflect on daily signs, renew prayer habits, and do a clear act of repentance and charity.

questions.text: What is one concluding takeaway about dunya from this Surah?
questions.answer: Dunya’s order is a sign, not a home—its stability ends, and the Hereafter begins.

questions.text: What is one concluding takeaway about akhirah from this Surah?
questions.answer: The appointed Day will come, and outcomes will be irreversible.

questions.text: What is the 'Great News' (An-Naba al-'Adhim) mentioned in the beginning?
questions.answer: The News of the Resurrection and the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: How does this Surah describe the earth and mountains?
questions.answer: The earth as a bed/expanse and the mountains as pegs (awtada).

questions.text: What is the purpose of sleep according to this Surah?
questions.answer: For rest (subata).

questions.text: How does this Surah describe the night and day?
questions.answer: The night as a covering and the day for livelihood (ma'asha).

questions.text: What are the 'seven strong [heavens]'?
questions.answer: The seven layers of the sky.

questions.text: What is the 'burning lamp' in the sky?
questions.answer: The sun.

questions.text: From where does the 'pouring water' come to produce grain and gardens?
questions.answer: From the rain-clouds (mu'sirat).

questions.text: What is the 'Day of Sorting' (Yawm al-Fasl)?
questions.answer: A day whose time is already appointed.

questions.text: What will happen when the Trumpet is blown?
questions.answer: People will come in crowds (afwaja).

questions.text: How will the heaven and mountains change on that Day?
questions.answer: The heaven will be opened as gates, and the mountains will be moved and become a mirage.

questions.text: Who is Hellfire waiting for as a 'place of return'?
questions.answer: The transgressors (Tagheen).

questions.text: What will the transgressors NOT taste in Hell?
questions.answer: Coolness or any drink, except for scalding water and foul purulence (Ghassaqa).

questions.text: Why are the people in Hell punished so severely?
questions.answer: Because they did not expect an account and denied Allah's signs completely.

questions.text: How does Allah keep track of deeds?
questions.answer: Everything is enumerated/recorded in a Book.

questions.text: What will the people in Hell be told to taste?
questions.answer: 'So taste! Never will We increase you except in punishment'.

questions.text: What is the 'Success' (Mafaza) for the righteous?
questions.answer: Gardens, grapevines, well-matched companions, and a full cup (of drink).

questions.text: Will there be any vain talk or lying in Paradise?
questions.answer: No.

questions.text: Who will stand in rows on that Day and not speak except by permission?
questions.answer: The Spirit (Jibreel) and the angels.

questions.text: What is the 'True Day'?
questions.answer: The Day of Judgment; so whoever wills should take a return to his Lord.

questions.text: What will the disbeliever say at the end of the Surah when he sees his deeds?
questions.answer: 'Oh, I wish that I were dust!'.
